courses.name: Grammar
courses.purpose: This course aims to take students' English grammar knowledge to the B2+ level and enable them to use the knowledge gained in reading, writing, and speaking skills effectively.
courses.description: The course teaches the functions and uses of English grammatical structures from simple to complex, helping students understand language structures and the relationships between them. Advanced English grammar functions and structures are examined to raise students' awareness of meaning-making and the use of these structures in different contexts.
learning_outcomes: They can understand and distinguish simple, compound, and complex sentences in English and use them easily in reading, writing, and speaking skills.
They can recognize the use of English grammar structures in different contexts.
They can define the functions of grammar structures and distinguish units with similar functions and structures.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: SUBJECT PRONOUNS (ÖZNE ZAMİRLERİ) PRESENT FORM TO BE (GENİŞ ZAMANDA TO BE) POSSESSIVES (İYELİK DURUMU)
Hafta 2: SIMPLE PRESENT TENSE (GENİŞ ZAMAN) THE PRESENT CONTINUOUS TENSE (ŞİMDİKİ ZAMAN)
Hafta 3: IMPERATIVES (EMİR CÜMLELERİ) DEMONSTRATIVES ADJECTIVES AND PRONOUNS (İŞARET SIFATLARI VE ZAMİRLERİ) & THERE IS – THERE ARE
Hafta 4: THE SIMPLE PAST TENSE (GEÇMİŞ ZAMAN THE SIMPLE PAST NEGATIVE STATEMENTS AND QUESTIONS (GEÇMİŞ ZAMAN OLUMSUZ VE SORU CÜMLELERİ) USED TO THE PAST CONTINUOUS TENSE (SÜREKLİ GEÇMİŞ ZAMAN)
Hafta 5: FUTURE TENSES (GELECEK ZAMAN) THE PRESENT PERFECT TENSE (YAKIN GEÇMİŞ ZAMAN)
Hafta 6: ADJECTIVES AND ADVERBS (SIFATLAR VE ZARFLAR) COMPARATIVE AND SUPERLATIVE (KARŞILAŞTIRMA VE ÜSTÜNLÜK)
Hafta 8: NOUNS AND ARTICLES (İSİMLİKLER VE TANIMIKLAR) QUANTIFIERS (MİKTAR BELİRLEYİCİLER)
Hafta 9: PRONOUNS (ZAMİRLER) PASSIVE VOICE ( EDİLGEN ÇATILI FİİLLER)
Hafta 11: GERUND & INFINITIVES (İSİM FİİLLER VE MASTARLAR) PREPOSITIONAL AND PHRASAL VERBS (EDATLI VE ÖBEKLİ FİİLLER)
Hafta 12: QUESTIONS AND ANSWERS (SORULAR VE CEVAPLAR) RELATIVE CLAUSE I ( SIFAK CÜMLECİKLERİ I )
Hafta 13: RELATIVE CLAUSES II (SIFAT CÜMLECİKLERİ II) NOUN CLAUSES (İSİM CÜMLECİKLERİ)
Hafta 14: REPORTED SPEECH (DOLAYLI ANLATIM) LINKING WORDS (TEMEL BAĞLAÇLAR)

courses.description: This course is designed to help students develop their English listening and speaking skills.
learning_outcomes: Students will improve their ability to understand English conversations in different contexts.
Students will be able to use English fluently and accurately in daily life.
Students will gain the ability to communicate effectively on various topics.

Hafta 8: Unit 8 | Getting Around the City

Students' improvement of academic reading and writing
Reading and writing accurately in the target language

courses.name: Calculus I
courses.purpose: This course aims to enable students to gain proficiency in mathematical calculations.
courses.description: A branch of science that deals with the calculation of instantaneous rates of change (differential calculus) and the summation of infinitely many small factors to determine a whole (integral calculus).
learning_outcomes: Learns the concept and types of functions
Can perform limit calculation and continuity analysis
Gains the ability to calculate and interpret derivatives
Gains the ability to graph functions
Can use the concept of differential
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Functions: Polynomials, Piecewise Functions, Exponential and Logarithmic Functions
Hafta 2: Trigonometric Functions, Inverse Trigonometric Functions
Hafta 3: Hyperbolic and Inverse Hyperbolic Functions
Hafta 6: Some Properties of Limits, Right-Hand and Left-Hand Limits
Hafta 8: Derivative: Definition and Basic Properties
Hafta 11: Maximum and Minimum of Functions
resources: Calculus, early transcendentals - James Stewart
Calculus - Wiley - Howard Anton, Irl C. Bivens, Stephen Davis
Calculus, early transcendentals - David E. Penney; Henry C. Edwards

courses.name: Chemistry
courses.purpose: The main objective of General Chemistry is to provide the fundamental understanding on chemistry concepts and methodology qualitatively. General Chemistry Students should have the ability of evaluation between chemistry and enginnering application in their daily life.
courses.description: A basic course emphasizing the metric system, introduction to stoichiometry, the structural and physical properties of matter, i.e., electronic structure of atoms, chemical binding, molecular geometry, hybridization and molecular orbitals and states of matter, i.e., gases, liquids and solids.
learning_outcomes: Student will be able to solve problems by using dimensional analysis for unit conversion.
Student will infer, based on atomic molecular theory, that the ratio of the masses of the elements forming a compound is always the same.
Student will be able to relate quantities (measured mass of substance, solution volumes or volumes and pressures of gases) to determine stoichiometric relationships in a reaction.
Student will be able to express the law of conservation of mass quantitatively and qualitatively using symbolic representations and special drawings.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to General Chemistry and Discussion of the Course Syllabus
Hafta 2: Fundamentals of Chemistry, Properties of Materials, Physical and Chemical Changes
Hafta 4: Chemical Reactions and Reaction Stoichiometry
Hafta 5: Chemical Reactions and Reaction Stoichiometry
Hafta 10: Aqueous Solutions and Reactions in Aqueous Solutions
Hafta 11: Ion Exchange Reactions and Gas Formation Reactions
Hafta 14: Gases and Molecular Kinetic Theory
resources: General Chemistry: The Essential Concepts, 5th Edition (Raymond Chang)

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: The goal of the course is to present a general introduction and overview of computer engineering.
courses.description: This course gives a brief overview of important aspects of computer science, including fundamentals in algorithms, binary data representation, Boolean logic, systems, software, networking concepts, introductory programming, computer organization, artificial intelligence, databases, control systems, and selected computer science subfields.
learning_outcomes: To understand basic concept of computer science
To provide students with the skills necessary to use computer systems
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: What is Computer Hardware and Software
resources: Invitation to Computer Science, G. M. Schneider and J. L. Gersting (Main Book)
Discovering Computers 2003: Concepts for a Digital, Shelly Cashman and Vermaat (Reference Book)
Computer Science, An Interdisciplinary Approach, Robert Sedgewick, Kevin Wayne Wesley Professional (Reference Book)
http://ceng.metu.edu.tr/ceng111

courses.name: Introduction to Information Technologies
courses.purpose: To introduce information technologies, provide information about their general principles, demonstrate the use of office applications such as operating systems, word processing, spreadsheets, and presentation software (MS Word, MS Excel, PowerPoint, etc.), and convey the importance of internet applications in professional work.
courses.description: Introduction to basic information technologies, Microsoft Windows topics, Microsoft Word topics, Microsoft PowerPoint topics, Microsoft Excel topics, the basic components that make up the Internet (internet protocols, web page, URL, ISP, IP address, internet address, etc.), use of Internet Explorer, and use of electronic mail.
learning_outcomes: Recognizes and defines basic information technology tools and concepts.
Recognizes basic computer hardware and software.
Uses at least one operating system.
Can perform text editing. Uses the Word word processing program.
Organizes numerical data. Uses the Excel spreadsheet program.
Prepares presentation materials. Uses the PowerPoint presentation program.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Definitions of informatics and information technologies.
Hafta 2: Computers: Yesterday, Today and Tomorrow
Hafta 3: Discussion of the hardware and software of the computer, the information technology tool used in our lessons. How do computers work?
Hafta 4: Discussion of the hardware and software of the computer, the information technology tool used in our lessons. Input, output and storage hardware.
Hafta 5: Discussion of the hardware and software of the computer, the information technology tool used in our lessons. System software.
Hafta 6: Discussion of the hardware and software of the computer, the information technology tool used in our lessons. Application software.

Hafta 7: Introduction and applications of the word processing program - creating a new document; selecting/cutting/copying/pasting; editing words, lines, sentences, paragraphs, sections and the entire document; using bullets and numbering; page formatting; spelling check; page view options.
Hafta 8: Course review. Questions about the applications.

Hafta 9: Equation editor, language change, spelling check, track changes. Adding page and section breaks, page numbers, pictures, symbols, footnotes and endnotes, text boxes, drawings, charts, headers and footers.
Hafta 10: Internet and World Wide Web and Web 2.0 and Cloud

Hafta 11: Excel Program: Introduction, view and customization, creating a new spreadsheet, saving, save as, page properties, working with cells, rows and columns, data entry, correction, moving, copying, paste special, adding, moving, copying, renaming worksheets, getting help, print preview and printing.

Hafta 12: Excel Program: Formatting features (font, color, borders, etc.) and changing them, formatting according to cell contents (number, text, date, etc.), autoformatting, conditional formatting. Working with formulas, arithmetic operator precedence, creating formulas. Adding and using functions, getting help about functions, sorting data, filtering. Drawing charts, chart types and areas of use, changing chart properties.

Hafta 14: PowerPoint Program: Introduction, view and customization, creating a new presentation, saving, adding, deleting, moving and copying slides, changing formatting properties, print preview and printing. Creating a design template using the master slide, changing existing design templates.
resources: Bilgisayara Giriş (Windows 7 ve Office 2010 Türkçe Sürümleri) Faruk Çubukçu (2011 yılı yayını), GAMA BASIM YAYIM DAĞITIM.
Computers Are Your Future, Introductory (12th Edition) by Catherine Laberta, Pearson.

courses.name: Introduction to Programming
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach the stages of computer-based problem solving, algorithm development, flowchart drawing, the structure of the MATLAB programming language, and the basic rules of computer programming.

Learns the basic logic of computer programming.
Learns the structure and syntax rules of the MATLAB programming language.
Writes, tests, finds errors in, and debugs computer programs using the MATLAB programming language.

Applies these topics

courses.name: Discrete Mathematics
courses.purpose: To teach the mathematical foundations of computer applications, to model information and to give the basics of graph theory.
courses.description: Discrete mathematics course aims to understand the abstract discrete structures that form the backbone of computer science. It includes topics such as sets, proof methods, functions, probability, graph theory, which have a significant impact on computer engineering applications.
learning_outcomes: The student can understand Sets, Propositions and Quantifiers.
The student can use mathematical proof methods.
The student can write algorithms and interpret given algorithms.
The student gain the ability to use graph theory in modeling discrete systems.
The student can make combinational circuit analysis.

Hafta 3: Arguments and Rules of Inference
Hafta 8: Counting Methods and the Pigeonhole Principle

courses.name: Linear Algebra
courses.purpose: The objective of this course is to provide students with a solid foundation in the fundamental concepts and techniques of linear algebra. Topics include systems of linear equations, matrix operations, vector spaces, determinants, eigenvalues, and eigenvectors. Emphasis will be placed on both theoretical understanding and practical applications in science and engineering. By the end of the course, students will be able to model and solve real-world problems using linear algebraic methods and develop critical thinking skills relevant to advanced mathematical reasoning.
courses.description: This course teaches Computer Engineering students to transfer their knowledge of Linear Algebra, to increase their reasoning power, and to teach mathematical concepts that they will use in the department.
learning_outcomes: The student learns the concept of vector and recognizes the operations that can be done on vectors.
The student understands matrices, systems of linear equations, determinants, vector spaces.
Student learns the application areas of Matrix and Determinants.
The student understands how to solve systems of Linear Equations.
The student learns what the concept of vector spaces is.
The student learns linear dependence and linear independence and reinforces their use.
The student learns the concepts of base, size and rank.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Image Processing with Matrix Operations
Hafta 7: Applications of Systems of Linear Equations
Hafta 12: Spanning Sets and Linear Independence
Hafta 14: Rank of a Matrix and Systems of Linear Equations
resources: R. Larson, D. C. Falvo, Elementary Linear Algebra, HOUGHTON MIFFLIN HARCOURT PUBLISHING COMPANY, 2009.
Jeffrey R. Chasnov, Matrix Algebra for Engineers, The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology, 2019.

Writes, tests, and debugs object-oriented console applications using the C++ programming language.

courses.name: Atatürk's Principles and History of Turkish Revolution I
courses.purpose: To introduce the concept of revolution and the Turkish Revolution, explain the collapse of the Ottoman Empire, and examine the National Struggle period, including the wars, congresses, relations with states, agreements, and the Treaty of Lausanne.

Students can understand the importance of the awakening initiated under Atatürk's leadership by the Turkish Nation against the collapse of the Ottoman Empire at the end of World War I and the occupation of the country.
Students can gain the ability to recognize, use, and apply various written and visual sources, materials, and documents related to these topics.

courses.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
courses.purpose: This course aims to introduce data structures and algorithms, enabling students to understand fundamental and advanced data structures as well as various types of algorithms. Additionally, it teaches different problem-solving approaches and the analysis of algorithm efficiency, helping students develop proficiency in algorithm design and analysis.
courses.description: This course introduces data structures and algorithms, focusing on fundamental and advanced concepts, problem-solving approaches, and algorithm efficiency analysis to enhance students' proficiency in algorithm design.
learning_outcomes: Students are introduced to basic data structures such as integers, floating-point numbers, characters, and booleans, as well as user-defined data structures like struct, union, and bit-level access.
Students learn to implement and analyze linear data structures such as arrays and linked lists.
Students gain proficiency in working with queues as a linear data structure.
Students develop a deep understanding of stack data structures and their applications.
Students learn about non-linear data structures, focusing on trees and their various types.
Students gain proficiency in working with graph data structures and their applications in real-world problems.
Students develop the ability to implement and analyze various sorting, searching, and problem-solving algorithms.
Students learn different algorithmic approaches, including recursion, dynamic programming, greedy algorithms, backtracking, and divide and conquer.
Students gain the ability to analyze the time and space complexity of algorithms to optimize performance in computational problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Data Structures and Algorithms / Basic Data Structures - Integer, Floating-Point Numbers, Characters, Boolean / User-Defined Data Structures - Struct, Union, Bit-Level Acces
Hafta 2: Linear Data Structures - Arrays, Linked List
Hafta 6: Non-Linear Data Structure - Binary Search Trees
Hafta 7: Non-Linear Data Structure - Balanced Search Trees
Hafta 9: Sorting Algorithms - Insertion Sort, Selection Sort, Bubble Sort
Hafta 10: Sorting Algorithms - Merge Sort, Heap Sort, Quick Sort
Hafta 11: Search Algorithms - Linear Search, Binary Search
Hafta 12: Algorithms According to Solution Approaches - Recursive Algorithm, Dynamic Programming, Greedy Algorithm
Hafta 13: Algorithms According to Solution Approaches - Backtracking Algorithm, Divide and Conquer Algorithm, Brute Force Algorithm
resources: Data Structures – A Pseudocode Approach with C, R. Gillberg, B. Forouzan, Thomson Course Technology Second Edition

courses.name: Differential Equations
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to enable students to model change processes frequently encountered in physics, engineering, and mathematics using ordinary differential equations and to solve these equations by analytical methods. It also aims to develop mathematical thinking, problem-solving, and abstraction skills, and to help students understand the theoretical structure of differential equations and adapt them correctly to applied problems.
courses.description: This course focuses on first-order ordinary differential equations and their basic solution methods. It covers separable equations, linear equations, exact differential equations, Bernoulli and homogeneous equations, as well as initial value problems and their geometric interpretation. The course is supported by many examples and applications to show the role of differential equations in mathematical modeling.
learning_outcomes: Adopt the concept of differential equations.
Understand the formation of differential equations.
Understand models related to the field.
Derive a given differential equation from its solution.
Solve different types of differential equations.
Gain the ability to solve initial and boundary value problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Basic Concepts in Differential Equations
Hafta 2: Finding Differential Equations from the General Solution
Hafta 7: Differential Equations Reducible to Exact Form
Hafta 9: Bernoulli Differential Equation, Riccati Differential Equation
Hafta 12: Method of Variation of Parameters, Cauchy-Euler Differential Equation
Hafta 13: Laplace and Inverse Laplace Transforms
Hafta 14: Solving Initial Value Problems by the Laplace Transform Method
resources: ADI DİFERENSİYEL DENKLEMLER-I DERS NOTLARI, PROF. DR. MURAT SUBAŞI
Differential Equations with Boundary-Value Problems, Seventh Edition, Dennis G. Zill and Michael D. Cullen

courses.name: Logic Design
courses.purpose: To learn the basic information about the logical design of digital circuits. Gaining the ability to look at events from a logical point of view. Gain the ability to divide complex problems into smaller parts and solve them more easily. It is the development of thought and theoretical knowledge that will form the basis for digital system design in higher grades.

Knowledge of number systems
Have knowledge about digital circuit design
Have knowledge about the process of digital circuits

courses.name: Programming Languages
courses.purpose: The aim of this course is to provide students with knowledge and skills in designing programming languages using modern design methods and implementing the designed languages using modern development tools.
courses.description: This course provides students with programming languages, example programming languages, syntactic and semantic analysis, names and scope, data types, arrays, arithmetic operations, control structures, subprograms, abstraction and encapsulation, object oriented programming language categories, parallel operation, error catching, and functional programming. The design and implementation principles of programming languages, language syntax, language processors (compilers, interpreters), forms of representation (data structures, control structures and connectors) and styles (procedural, functional programming, logic programming, modular programming, object-oriented programming) constitute the content of the course.
learning_outcomes: Explaining paradigms in programming languages
Adequate knowledge of programming languages; to be able to apply theoretical and applied knowledge in these fields for modeling and solving engineering problems.
Ability to choose the appropriate programming language after analysis
Ability to categorize the given programming language
To be able to explain, use and compare program structures in different programming languages.
To be able to effectively use the current technologies required for the design and implementation of programming languages.

Hafta 2: Programming Languages Concepts, Programming Domains, Language Evaluation Criteria, Factors Affecting Language Design, Language Categories, Language Design Concessions, Implementation Methods, Programming Platforms
Hafta 3: Development of Important Programming Languages
Hafta 6: Names, Binding, Type Checking, and Scopes

courses.name: Design and Analysis of Algorithms

Can write and run shared-memory parallel programs using C++ and OpenMP in Linux.

Hafta 4: High Performance Computing (HPC) systems and introduction to parallel programming, HPC with cluster architecture, serial computation, shared memory model, distributed memory model, parallel computation models, speedup, Amdahl's Law, principle of locality.

Hafta 5: Introduction to distributed computing with MPI, SMPD model (Single Program Multiple Data), environment management routines, compiling and running C++ programs containing MPI routines (mpiicpc and mpirun programs), "Hello World" example, applications.
Hafta 6: Writing and running MPI applications in the Windows environment using Microsoft Visual Studio 2010 and existing MPICH2 programs for Windows, applications.
Hafta 7: Running an MPI-written parallel C++ console application on MPICH2-installed computers within a local network, applications.

courses.name: Applied Machine Learning
courses.purpose: This course aims to teach students the theoretical and practical foundations of supervised learning. Students will learn to build probabilistic and discriminative models, optimize these models with gradient-based methods, evaluate model performance, and understand the strengths and weaknesses of various supervised learning techniques.
courses.description: This is an introductory course that comprehensively presents the fundamental concepts and methods of supervised learning. It covers topics such as basic probability theory, maximum likelihood estimation, logistic regression, fundamentals of artificial neural networks, optimization algorithms, evaluation metrics, and support vector machines. Through theoretical foundations and applied examples, the course aims to provide students with a strong background in building, training, and evaluating supervised learning models. By the end of the course, students will understand how predictive models are formulated, optimized, and validated.
learning_outcomes: Explain the basic concepts, scope, and learning paradigms of artificial intelligence
Apply data preparation processes such as cleaning, normalization, feature selection, and feature extraction
weekly_topics: Hafta 8: Introduction to Artificial Neural Networks

courses.description: Definition of machine learning, machine learning concepts and approaches (sample and hypothesis space, concept learning, decision tree learning, artificial neural networks, Bayesian learning, support vector machines, genetic algorithms, instance-based learning), and the role of machine learning within data mining.

To define the structure and components of a Python program. To learn how to write loops and decision statements in Python. To learn how to write functions and pass arguments in Python. To learn how to build and package Python modules for reusability. To learn how to read and write files in Python. To learn how to design object-oriented programs with Python classes. To learn how to use class inheritance in Python for reusability. To learn how to use exception handling in Python applications for error handling.

Develop Python code for very popular statistical methods

courses.name: Applied Unsupervised Learning Methods
courses.purpose: This course aims to provide students with a deep understanding of unsupervised learning methods and to develop their ability to use these methods in data analytics and modeling processes. It also introduces the theoretical foundations of various algorithms while demonstrating their practical applications on different datasets.
courses.description: This course covers the fundamental principles of unsupervised learning methods that operate on unlabeled data and their applied use. Students will learn to use unsupervised learning approaches such as clustering, dimensionality reduction, and anomaly detection on different datasets. They will also gain in-depth knowledge of how to apply unsupervised algorithms to solve problems encountered in data analysis and how to evaluate their performance.
learning_outcomes: Explains the basic principles and working mechanisms of unsupervised learning algorithms.
Applies unsupervised learning techniques such as clustering and dimensionality reduction to real-world datasets.
Analyzes unlabeled datasets and identifies hidden patterns and structures in the data.
Compares different unsupervised learning algorithms and determines which methods are more suitable for which types of problems.
Evaluates the advantages and limitations of unsupervised learning methods and integrates them into analytical processes.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: The place of unsupervised learning in machine learning, differences between supervised and unsupervised learning, general overview of unsupervised learning problems.
Hafta 2: Structure and characteristics of unlabeled data, data cleaning and preprocessing techniques, need for dimensionality reduction and data transformations.
Hafta 3: Theoretical foundations of the K-Means algorithm, performance metrics for clustering.
Hafta 4: K-Means applications and real-world examples.
Hafta 7: Theoretical foundations and mathematical background of PCA, data compression and dimensionality reduction.
Hafta 9: Applied use of PCA and interpretation of results.
Hafta 10: Comparison of methods and success analysis.
resources: Applied Unsupervised Learning Methods

courses.name: Introduction to Artificial Intelligence
courses.purpose: Bu dersin amacı, öğrencilere yapay zekânın temel prensiplerini ve veri odaklı yöntemlerini öğretmektir. Öğrencilerin makine öğrenmesi modellerinin nasıl çalıştığını kavramaları, çeşitli sınıflandırma, kümeleme ve ilişki çıkarımı tekniklerini uygulayabilmeleri, veri hazırlama ve model değerlendirme süreçlerini anlayabilmeleri hedeflenmektedir. Ders sonunda öğrencilerin yapay zekâ problemlerini analiz edebilme, uygun çözüm yöntemleri seçme ve temel yapay zekâ modelleri geliştirme becerisi kazanmaları amaçlanmaktadır.
courses.description: Bu ders, yapay zekânın temel kavramlarını, yöntemlerini ve uygulama alanlarını kapsamlı bir şekilde tanıtan giriş düzeyinde bir derstir. Derste veri hazırlama, temel sınıflandırma yöntemleri, karar ağaçları, lojistik regresyon, Naive Bayes sınıflandırıcıları, kümeleme teknikleri, k-means algoritması, optimizasyon yöntemleri, birliktelik kuralı çıkarımı ve öneri sistemleri gibi modern veri odaklı yapay zekâ yaklaşımları ele alınacaktır. Teorik bilgiler, örnek uygulamalar ve pratik problem çözme süreçleriyle desteklenerek öğrencilerin yapay zekâ tekniklerini anlamaları ve temel modelleri uygulayabilmeleri hedeflenmektedir.
learning_outcomes: Yapay zekânın temel kavramlarını, kapsamını ve öğrenme paradigmalarını açıklayabilir
Veri hazırlama süreçlerini (temizleme, normalizasyon, özellik seçimi ve çıkarımı) uygulayabilir.
resources: Christopher M. Bishop, Pattern Recognition and Machine Learning, Springer, 2006.

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Vision

courses.description: Computer Vision and Fundamental Concepts. Human Visual Perception. Mathematical Foundations. Image Formation and Representation. Object and Human Tracking. Human Activity Recognition and Understanding.

Understands motion detection methods in video sequences.
Has knowledge of object and human tracking.
Comprehends activity recognition and inference techniques.
